panels.description: Wide shot: The Longbourn drawing room is bathed in the cold light of a cloudy day. Elizabeth stands near a bookshelf, looking toward Charlotte who is seated by the window; both avoid eye contact. Mr. Bennet is seen in the background with a scowl, leaning against the mantle. The atmosphere is thick with palpable tension and silence.

bubbles.text: A cold restraint grew between Elizabeth and Charlotte, a silence that signaled the end of their true confidence.

panels.description: Medium shot: Elizabeth and Jane stand close together in a hallway. Elizabeth looks at Jane with deep affection and concern, her hand resting on Jane's arm. Jane looks out toward the front door, her expression hopeful but weary as she clutches a small, folded letter. Soft, natural light from a side window.

bubbles.text: With her trust in Charlotte broken, Elizabeth turned to Jane, whose anxiety for Bingley's return grew with every passing day.

panels.description: Medium shot: Mr. Bennet in his study, holding a heavy, formal letter with a prominent wax seal. He has a cynical, amused smirk. The background is filled with stacks of old books and a desk cluttered with quills and ink. Dust motes dance in a single beam of light.

bubbles.text: A letter from Mr. Collins arrived, announcing with pompous gratitude his engagement to their neighbor, Miss Lucas.

panels.description: Medium close-up: Mrs. Bennet is in the center of the drawing room, her face flushed red with indignation. She is gesturing dramatically with a silk handkerchief. Her eyes are wide and watery, conveying exaggerated distress. The background shows the ornate but slightly cluttered interior of Longbourn.

bubbles.text: It is very strange he should come here instead of Lucas Lodge! I hate having visitors while my health is so indifferent!
Her murmurs of complaint only gave way to the greater distress of Bingley's continued absence.

panels.description: Close-up: Elizabeth's face is partially in shadow as she gazes out a rainy window. Her expression is one of deep calculation and dread. In the reflection of the glass, we see a faint, stylized silhouette of the Bingley sisters and Mr. Darcy standing together in a sophisticated London setting, symbolizing her fears.

bubbles.text: Elizabeth feared the united efforts of his unfeeling sisters and overpowering friend would be too much for Bingley's attachment.

panels.description: Medium shot: Mrs. Bennet leans over Jane, who is trying to read a book. Mrs. Bennet is talking incessantly, her mouth open and a finger pointed for emphasis. Jane looks down at her book with a forced, calm expression, her shoulders slightly hunched as if enduring a storm.

bubbles.text: Jane, you must confess! If he does not come back, you are very ill-used!

panels.description: Wide shot: The exterior of Longbourn. Mr. Collins is stepping out of a carriage, looking extremely self-satisfied and smiling broadly. Charlotte Lucas stands nearby, greeting him with a polite, reserved smile. The rest of the Bennet family is visible in the doorway, looking weary and unenthusiastic.

bubbles.text: Mr. Collins returned punctually. Luckily for the family, his 'business of love-making' with Charlotte relieved them of his company.
